description:
You are a technical writer who has just created three placeholder Markdown files for a new project.  
    • The files already exist at the following absolute paths:  
      1. /home/user/docs/faq.md  
      2. /home/user/docs/introduction.md  
      3. /home/user/docs/usage-guide.md  

    For version-control and integrity purposes, you must generate a SHA-256 checksum list for every “.md” file that lives directly inside /home/user/docs (do not recurse into sub-directories; there are none right now).  
    Your goal is to create one new file, located at  

        /home/user/docs/checksums.txt  

    The file must satisfy ALL of these formatting requirements, because an automated test will check them byte-for-byte:

    1. Each line must contain exactly two space characters between the 64-character hexadecimal digest and the filename (例: “e3...855␠␠faq.md”).  
    2. Use only the base filename (e.g., “faq.md”), **not** the full path.  
    3. Lines must be sorted in ascending, case-sensitive ASCII order by filename.  
    4. The file must contain one trailing newline after the last line (i.e., it is a typical Unix text file).  
    5. No extra blank lines or leading/trailing spaces are allowed anywhere.  

    In short, the final /home/user/docs/checksums.txt must have three lines—one for faq.md, one for introduction.md, and one for usage-guide.md—listed in that exact order and produced with the SHA-256 algorithm.

    Hint: a single standard GNU/Linux command can calculate all the hashes and write them to a file in the correct order; you may find the utilities “sha256sum” and “sort” useful.  

    Once done, you do not need to display the file on screen; just make sure it exists at the specified path meeting the format above.
truth:
Initial state supplied to the container before the agent starts:
    • Directory /home/user/docs already exists and is writable by the user running the agent.
    • Files present and **empty** (0 bytes each):
        - /home/user/docs/faq.md
        - /home/user/docs/introduction.md
        - /home/user/docs/usage-guide.md

    SHA-256 digest of an empty file (and therefore of every .md file in this task):
        e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

    Expected final file created by the agent:
        Path: /home/user/docs/checksums.txt
        Permissions: 0644 (-rw-r--r--)
        Exact byte-for-byte contents (including the terminal newline):

e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  faq.md
e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  introduction.md
e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  usage-guide.md
<newline>

    (The literal “<newline>” above represents the single trailing “\n” character.)

    The automated test will:
    1. Verify /home/user/docs/checksums.txt exists.
    2. Compare its contents byte-for-byte against the block shown above.
